title: Private costs and benefits, externalities and social costs and benefits
paperName: Cambridge International A Level Economics (P3 & P4)
summary: 9708 A Level externalities — MPC, MSC, MPB, MSB, and welfare analysis with GeoGebra.
learningObjectives: Distinguish private and social costs and benefits.
Analyse negative and positive externalities on diagrams.

simpleExplanation.title: Spillover costs and benefits
simpleExplanation.summary: Externalities cause a wedge between private and social curves. Negative externalities lead to overproduction relative to the social optimum.
steps: Pigouvian tax = external cost per unit at optimum.

sections.content: **Externalities** (7.4) are core A Level diagram work. Shade **DWL** and label **Q_market** vs **Q_social** on every answer.

steps.caption: MPC private cost; MSC = MPC + external cost.
MPB private benefit; MSB = MPB + external benefit.
Overproduction when negative externality — MSC > MSB at market Q.
Pigouvian tax/subsidy moves Q toward social optimum.
steps.embedHint: Tax per unit = external cost at optimum.
